capabilities: Apply content hashing to attribute knowledge in database optimization, ensuring operations are error-free and logged for auditing.

procedure.action: Execute content hashing to database optimization queries by generating SHA-256 hashes for query strings; store hashes in a dedicated metadata table for attribution by executing the transformation function on each input record; assert output_schema_valid = true and error_count < 1; log transformation_duration_ms.
Execute optimized queries and assert error_count < 1 by checking the database error logs; ensure result_valid = true by verifying query results against expected outputs.

Log operation_duration_ms and result_code for each query execution to an audit trail table, ensuring traceability and accountability of database operations using domain-appropriate tooling; assert error_count < 1 and result_valid = true; log operation_duration_ms and result_code to audit trail.
Reapply content hashing after query optimization changes by recalculating SHA-256 hashes; update the metadata table with new hashes to maintain accurate attribution.
Assert the procedure output by running a consistency check on the metadata table; assert error_count < 1 and result_valid = true by comparing stored hashes with recalculated ones.

summary: Optimize database operations by attributing knowledge using content hashes, ensuring error-free execution and logging for audit trails.
title: Database Optimization

failureConditions: Inconsistent query results when content hashes are not updated after optimization, causing mismatches in attribution.
Audit trail gaps when operation_duration_ms and result_code are not logged, leading to accountability issues.
Error-prone execution when error_count is not checked, causing undetected failures in database operations.
metrics: error_count < 1 by checking the database error logs
the procedure output by running a consistency check on the metadata table
error_count < 1 and result_valid = true by comparing stored hashes with recalculated ones
